jobs.title: Client Services Officer
jobs.description:
# Client Services Officer

**Southern Cross Financial** | Perth, WA | Full-time

---

## About the Role

Join our Client Experience team as we enhance service delivery for our valued clients across Western Australia. Reporting to Lisa Kwan, Client Experience & Marketing Manager, you'll be the first point of contact for our high-net-worth individuals, SME owners, and corporate clients, ensuring every interaction reflects our commitment to personalised financial advice.

As part of a close-knit team of three in our Client Experience department, you'll play a crucial role in maintaining the trusted relationships that have defined Southern Cross Financial since 2011. This position offers excellent exposure to the financial services industry and opportunities to develop your career within our growing firm.

## Key Responsibilities

**Client Communication & Support**
- Serve as the primary point of contact for client enquiries via phone, email, and in-person visits
- Schedule appointments and manage calendars for our financial planning team
- Coordinate client meetings and prepare meeting rooms with necessary documentation
- Follow up on client requests and ensure timely resolution of enquiries

**Document Management & Administration**
- Maintain accurate client files and ensure compliance with ASIC record-keeping requirements
- Process new client onboarding documentation and KYC procedures
- Prepare and distribute client statements, reports, and correspondence
- Assist with the preparation of client review meetings and annual portfolio reports

**Operational Support**
- Support the Client Experience team with marketing initiatives and client events
- Maintain client database accuracy and generate reports as required
- Coordinate with our compliance team to ensure all client interactions meet regulatory standards
- Provide backup support for reception duties when required

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements**
- Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Commerce, or related field
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills with a professional telephone manner
- Strong attention to detail and ability to handle confidential information with discretion
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel and Outlook
- Customer service experience, preferably in a professional services environment

**Desirable Qualifications**
- Previous experience in financial services, banking, or professional services
- Understanding of financial products and services
- Experience with CRM systems and document management platforms
- Knowledge of compliance requirements in financial services

**Personal Attributes**
- Strong organisational skills and ability to prioritise multiple tasks
- Professional presentation and interpersonal skills
- Proactive approach to problem-solving and continuous improvement
- Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a small team

## What We Offer

**Professional Development**
- Comprehensive training in financial services and regulatory requirements
- Mentoring from experienced professionals in wealth management
- Support for industry-relevant professional development and certifications
- Clear pathways for career progression within our growing firm

**Benefits & Culture**
- Competitive salary package with performance-based reviews
- Flexible working arrangements to support work-life balance
- Modern office environment in Perth's CBD with on-site parking
- Team-focused culture with regular social events and team building activities
- Exposure to Western Australia's resource sector and high-net-worth client base

## How to Apply

To apply for this position, please submit your resume and cover letter outlining your interest in joining Southern Cross Financial and relevant experience in client service roles.

Applications should be submitted via email to careers@southerncrossfinancial.com.au with the subject line "Client Services Officer Application - [Your Name]".

We welcome applications from candidates who share our commitment to delivering exceptional client service and are looking to build their career in the financial services industry.

*Southern Cross Financial is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all team members.*

---

**About Southern Cross Financial**

Established in 2011, Southern Cross Financial is a leading financial services firm providing wealth management, financial planning, and corporate advisory services across Western Australia. We pride ourselves on delivering personalised advice and maintaining deep understanding of the WA resource sector wealth cycle, serving clients who value trusted relationships and expert guidance.

jobs.title: Compliance Analyst
jobs.description:
# Compliance Analyst

**Southern Cross Financial | Compliance & Risk | Perth, WA | Full-time**

Are you a recent graduate with a passion for financial regulation and risk management? Southern Cross Financial is seeking a detail-oriented Compliance Analyst to join our growing Compliance & Risk team as we navigate an increasingly complex regulatory landscape.

## About the Role

Reporting to Annabel Tan, Head of Compliance & Risk, you'll play a crucial role in ensuring our financial services operations meet ASIC requirements and industry best practices. This is an excellent opportunity for a law, finance, or business graduate to build expertise in financial services compliance while working directly with experienced advisers and senior management.

As we implement new regulatory frameworks following the Banking Royal Commission reforms and prepare for upcoming adviser education standards, you'll be at the forefront of maintaining our Australian Financial Services Licence obligations while supporting our mission to deliver personalised wealth management advice.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Adviser Conduct Monitoring**: Review adviser activities, client interactions, and advice documentation to ensure compliance with ASIC regulations and internal policies
- **File Reviews**: Conduct systematic reviews of client files, Statement of Advice documents, and ongoing service records to identify compliance gaps
- **Compliance Registers**: Maintain and update breach registers, training records, and regulatory correspondence databases
- **Documentation Support**: Assist with preparation of compliance reports, ASIC submissions, and internal audit documentation
- **Policy Implementation**: Help roll out new compliance procedures across our wealth management and lending teams
- **Training Assistance**: Support delivery of compliance training sessions for advisers and support staff
- **Regulatory Research**: Monitor ASIC updates, industry guidance, and regulatory changes affecting our operations

## What You'll Bring

- **Education**: Bachelor's degree in Law, Finance, Business, or related field
- **Attention to Detail**: Strong analytical skills with ability to identify inconsistencies and potential compliance issues
- **Communication**: Excellent written and verbal communication skills for interacting with advisers, clients, and regulators
- **Technology**: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with compliance management systems preferred
- **Initiative**: Self-motivated approach with ability to work independently while contributing to a small, collaborative team
- **Understanding**: Basic knowledge of financial services regulation (ASIC, APRA) and willingness to develop deep expertise
- **Integrity**: High ethical standards and commitment to maintaining client confidentiality

*Previous internship or work experience in financial services, law firms, or professional services environment highly regarded.*

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Package**: $65,000 - $75,000 plus superannuation, based on experience
- **Professional Development**: Full study support for relevant qualifications (RG146, Graduate Diploma of Applied Corporate Governance)
- **Career Growth**: Clear pathway to senior compliance roles as our business expands
- **Mentorship**: Direct exposure to senior management and experienced advisers across wealth management and corporate advisory
- **Flexible Work**: Hybrid working arrangements with focus on collaboration and client service
- **Team Environment**: Join a close-knit team of 55 professionals who value personal relationships and quality advice
- **Industry Exposure**: Gain insights into WA's resource sector wealth cycle and high-net-worth client management

## How to Apply

Send your resume and cover letter to careers@southerncrossfinancial.com.au, addressing how your background aligns with our compliance objectives and career aspirations in financial services.

**Applications close**: 15 December 2024

For questions about this role, contact Annabel Tan at a.tan@southerncrossfinancial.com.au

---

*Southern Cross Financial is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.*

jobs.title: Marketing & Content Coordinator
jobs.description:
# Marketing & Content Coordinator

**Department:** Client Experience & Marketing  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Part-time (20-25 hours per week)  
**Reports To:** Lisa Kwan, Client Experience & Marketing Manager

## About the Role

Southern Cross Financial is seeking a detail-oriented Marketing & Content Coordinator to join our growing Client Experience & Marketing team. This part-time role is perfect for a marketing or communications student looking to gain hands-on experience in the financial services sector while studying.

Working directly with Lisa Kwan, our Client Experience & Marketing Manager, you'll be responsible for creating engaging content across our digital channels while ensuring strict compliance with ASIC regulations and financial services marketing requirements. This is an excellent opportunity to develop your skills in regulated marketing, content creation, and client communication within Western Australia's leading boutique financial services firm.

As we navigate the evolving regulatory landscape and work to modernise our client experience while maintaining our personal touch, you'll play a key role in helping us communicate effectively with our high-net-worth individual and corporate clients across WA.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Content Creation:** Develop compelling content for monthly client newsletters, social media posts, website updates, and marketing materials that comply with financial services regulations
- **Regulatory Compliance:** Work closely with our Head of Compliance & Risk, Annabel Tan, to ensure all marketing materials meet ASIC disclosure requirements and industry standards
- **Social Media Management:** Maintain our LinkedIn and professional social media presence, engaging with clients and prospects while adhering to strict compliance guidelines
- **Website Support:** Update website content, case studies, and service descriptions to reflect our evolving service offerings and regulatory requirements
- **Campaign Support:** Assist with email marketing campaigns, client event promotions, and thought leadership initiatives
- **Market Research:** Monitor industry trends and competitor activities to inform content strategy and positioning
- **Administrative Support:** Maintain content calendars, coordinate with external suppliers, and support the broader marketing function

## What You'll Bring

- Currently enrolled in a marketing, communications, journalism, or related degree program
- Strong written communication skills with attention to detail and grammar
- Basic understanding of social media platforms and content management systems
- Interest in financial services and willingness to learn about regulatory requirements
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, or similar design tools preferred
- Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously
- Professional attitude and understanding of confidentiality requirements
- Availability to work 20-25 hours per week, with flexibility around university schedule

**Desirable but not essential:**
- Previous experience in financial services, professional services, or regulated industries
- Knowledge of ASIC regulations or compliance frameworks
- Experience with email marketing platforms and website content management

## What We Offer

- **Competitive hourly rate** of $28-32 per hour, depending on experience
- **Flexible working arrangements** that accommodate your university schedule
- **Professional development** opportunities including compliance training and industry education
- **Mentorship** from experienced marketing and compliance professionals
- **Real-world experience** in regulated marketing and financial services
- **Networking opportunities** within WA's financial services sector
- **Potential for increased hours** during university breaks
- **Possible pathway to graduate role** upon completion of studies

You'll be working in our modern Perth CBD office with a collaborative team of 55 professionals who are passionate about delivering exceptional financial advice to Western Australian clients.

## How to Apply

To apply, please submit your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in financial services marketing and how this role fits with your study and career goals. Include any relevant coursework, projects, or experience that demonstrates your writing ability and attention to detail.

Applications should be sent to [careers@southerncrossfinancial.com.au](mailto:careers@southerncrossfinancial.com.au) with the subject line "Marketing & Content Coordinator Application - [Your Name]".

We'll be conducting interviews on a rolling basis, so early applications are encouraged.

---

*Southern Cross Financial is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace where all team members can thrive.*

jobs.title: Paraplanner
jobs.description:
# Paraplanner

**Department:** Financial Planning  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time  
**Reports to:** Naomi Street, Head of Financial Planning

## About the Role

Southern Cross Financial is seeking a detail-oriented Paraplanner to join our growing Financial Planning team. As part of our 8-person planning division, you'll work directly with our senior financial planners to prepare comprehensive statements of advice and conduct investment research for our diverse client base of high-net-worth individuals and SME owners across Western Australia.

This is an excellent opportunity for a finance graduate to build expertise in financial planning while contributing to our firm's continued growth. You'll gain exposure to complex wealth management strategies, SMSF administration, and the unique financial needs of WA's resource sector professionals.

## Key Responsibilities

- Prepare detailed statements of advice (SOAs) and records of advice (ROAs) ensuring full compliance with ASIC regulatory requirements
- Conduct comprehensive research on investment products, platforms, and strategies across equities, managed funds, ETFs, and alternative investments
- Analyse client financial positions and model various scenarios using our financial planning software
- Prepare fact-finding questionnaires and client meeting documentation
- Support senior planners with portfolio reviews and rebalancing recommendations
- Maintain accurate client files and ensure all documentation meets compliance standards
- Assist with annual review processes and ongoing client communication
- Research superannuation and pension strategies, particularly for mining executives and business owners
- Stay current with regulatory changes and industry best practices through continuing professional development

## What You'll Bring

**Essential:**
- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Financial Planning, Economics, or related field
- Strong analytical skills with exceptional attention to detail
- Excellent written communication skills and ability to explain complex concepts clearly
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite, particularly Excel
- Understanding of Australian superannuation system and investment fundamentals
- Commitment to pursuing CFP certification or equivalent professional qualifications

**Highly Regarded:**
- Previous experience in financial services or planning (including internships)
- Knowledge of XPLAN or similar financial planning software
- Understanding of SMSF administration and compliance requirements
- Familiarity with Australian taxation and estate planning principles

## What We Offer

- Competitive starting salary of $65,000 - $75,000 plus superannuation
- Comprehensive professional development program and study support for CFP certification
- Mentorship from experienced financial planners including direct coaching from Naomi Street
- Opportunity to work with high-net-worth clients and complex wealth structures
- Clear career progression pathway to Associate Planner and beyond
- Collaborative team environment in our modern Perth CBD offices
- Flexible working arrangements including hybrid work options
- Access to industry conferences and training programs
- 25 days annual leave plus public holidays

## How to Apply

Submit your resume and cover letter explaining your interest in financial planning and what you'd bring to the Southern Cross Financial team. Please include any relevant coursework, internships, or projects that demonstrate your analytical abilities and attention to detail.

We're looking for someone who shares our commitment to providing personalised, high-quality financial advice while adapting to the evolving regulatory landscape.

**Applications close:** Friday, 15 March 2024

*Southern Cross Financial is an equal opportunity employer committed to workplace diversity.*
